mission.title: Install and Configure Cert Manager on Kubernetes
mission.description: cert-manager is a powerful tool that automates the provisioning and management of TLS certificates in Kubernetes. Installing cert-manager allows you to easily secure your applications with valid certificates, ensuring they remain up to date and reducing the risk of outages.

steps.title: Add the Jetstack Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Jetstack Helm repository which contains the cert-manager chart.
```bash
helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install cert-manager using Helm
steps.description:
Install cert-manager in the 'cert-manager' namespace with the specified version.
```bash
helm install cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager --version v1.19.4 --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --set installCRDs=true
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the cert-manager pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cert-manager
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for cert-manager
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the cert-manager deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment cert-manager --namespace cert-manager --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

steps.title: Set up a ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt
steps.description:
Create a ClusterIssuer resource to use Let's Encrypt for certificate issuance.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: ClusterIssuer
metadata:
  name: letsencrypt-prod
spec:
  acme:
    # You must replace this email address with your own.
    email: your-email@example.com
    server: https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
    privateKeySecretRef:
      name: letsencrypt-prod
    solvers:
    - http01:
        ingress:
          class: nginx
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of cert-manager will show the cert-manager pods in the 'cert-manager' namespace as running. Additionally, you will have a ClusterIssuer configured for obtaining TLS certificates from Let's Encrypt.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm repo update
helm install cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager --version v1.19.4 --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --set installCRDs=true

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: ClusterIssuer
metadata:
  name: letsencrypt-prod
spec:
  acme:
    # You must replace this email address with your own.
    email: your-email@example.com
    server: https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
    privateKeySecretRef:
      name: letsencrypt-prod
    solvers:
    - http01:
        ingress:
          class: nginx
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the cert-manager release from the 'cert-manager' namespace using Helm.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and any persistent resources associated with cert-manager.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'cert-manager' namespace and verify that all resources have been removed.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of cert-manager resources.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Jetstack Helm repository and upgrade cert-manager to the desired version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that the cert-manager pods are running the new version successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If cert-manager pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors using the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cert-manager
```

troubleshooting.title: Certificate issuance fails
troubleshooting.description:
If certificates are not being issued, check the status of the ClusterIssuer:
```bash
kubectl describe clusterissuer letsencrypt-prod
```

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
If the Ingress is not routing traffic correctly, verify the Ingress resource configuration:
```bash
kubectl describe ingress <ingress-name> --namespace cert-manager
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not installed
troubleshooting.description:
If the CRDs are missing, ensure they were installed during the Helm installation. You can check the CRDs with:
```bash
kubectl get crds | grep cert-manager.io
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
